DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Apperances: No appearances by both sides.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ROBERT ALVERNAZ, ERIN GRACE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend in part and sustained without leave to amend in part. A plaintiff asserting an elder abuse cause of action must allege facts establishing that the defendant denied or withheld goods or services necessary to meet the elder or dependent adult's basic needs, either with knowledge that injury was substantially certain to befall the elder or dependent adult or with conscious disregard of the high probability of such injury. Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 395, 406-407.) The facts constituting the neglect and establishing the causal link between the neglect and the injury must be pleaded with particularity, in accordance with the pleading rules governing statutory claims. (Ibid.) The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for elder abuse is sustained with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to plead specific facts which demonstrate that Defendants Erin Grace and Robert Alvernaz knew that Mr. Zebest had become agitated and as a result of his increased agitation posed a safety hazard to Plaintiff or specific facts which show that Defendants Erin Grace and Robert Alvernaz consciously disregarded the fact that Mr. Zebest had become agitated and the high probability of Mr. Zebest attacking Plaintiff. The demurrer to the seventh cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because the immunity statute clearly applies to Defendant City and County of San Francisco on the cause of action. (Guzman c. County of Los Angeles (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 1343.) Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Nancy B. Wong. Not Reported (611/LOT)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Apperances: No appearances by both sides.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:DEFENDANT DERRICK DEH-RHONG LUNG. M.D. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. A plaintiff asserting an elder abuse cause of action must allege facts establishing that the defendant denied or withheld goods or services necessary to meet the elder or dependent adult's basic needs, either with knowledge that injury was substantially certain to befall the elder or dependent adult or with conscious disregard of the high probability of such injury. Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 395, 406-407.) The facts constituting the neglect and establishing the causal link between the neglect and the injury must be pleaded with particularity, in accordance with the pleading rules governing statutory claims. (Ibid.) The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for elder abuse is sustained with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to plead specific facts which demonstrate that Defendant Dr. Derrick Lung knew that Mr. Zebest had become agitated and as a result of his increased agitation posed a safety hazard to Plaintiff or specific facts which show that Defendant Dr. Derrick Lung consciously disregarded the fact that Mr. Zebest had become agitated and the high probability of Mr. Zebest attacking Plaintiff. The demurrer to the seventh cause of action is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege facts identifying the acts or omissions that Defendant Dr. Derrick Lung engaged in which constitute an ultrahazardous activity. The issue of whether an activity is ultrahazardous is not to be decided on demurrer. (SKF Farms v. Superior Court (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 902, 906; Travelers Indem., Co. v. Reondo Beach (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1432.) Here, the demurrer to the seventh cause of action is sustained with leave to amend because there is uncertainty regarding what actions taken by Defendant Dr. Derrick Lung were ultrahazardous. In the second amended complaint Plaintiff merely alleges that all Defendants engaged in an ultrahazardous activity of maintaining control and custody of a person who was a known danger to others. (SAC paragraph 132.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Nancy B. Wong. Not Reported (611/LOT)

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MERY PEREZ'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amendment To Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint is granted. In light of the liberal policy which courts follow in permitting parties to file amendments to the pleadings, Plaintiff's motion for leave to file an amendment to the second amended complaint is granted. RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Derrick Deh-Rhong Lung, M.D. ("Lung") Motion to Compel Compliance with Subpoena for Orin Zebest's Records is GRANTED as unopposed.
